privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.description: Learn how we protect your data

q1.question: What number formats does Bit Scope support?
q1.answer: Bit Scope supports 8-bit, 16-bit, 32-bit, and 64-bit integers, as well as Float16, Float32, and Float64 floating-point formats with full IEEE 754 compliance.

q2.question: Can I perform bitwise operations?
q2.answer: Yes! Bit Scope supports AND, OR, XOR, and NOT bitwise operations. You can use a second operand (B) to perform operations with your primary value (A).

q3.question: What number bases are supported?
q3.answer: Bit Scope supports Binary (BIN), Octal (OCT), Decimal (DEC), and Hexadecimal (HEX) number bases. You can input and display values in any of these bases with real-time conversion.

q4.question: Does Bit Scope work offline?
q4.answer: Yes! Bit Scope works completely offline. No internet connection is required. All calculations are performed locally on your device using BigInt for integers and IEEE 754 standards for floating-point numbers.

q5.question: What is the IEEE 754 breakdown feature?
q5.answer: The IEEE 754 breakdown provides a visual representation of floating-point number components, showing the sign bit, exponent, and mantissa separately. This is helpful for understanding how floating-point numbers are stored and represented.

q6.question: Can I change endianness and bit order?
q6.answer: Yes! Bit Scope allows you to switch between Big Endian (BE) and Little Endian (LE) byte ordering, as well as choose between MSB (Most Significant Bit) and LSB (Least Significant Bit) bit order for display.

terms.title: Terms of Use
terms.intro: Please read these Terms of Use carefully before using Bit Scope. By accessing or using our service, you agree to be bound by these terms. If you do not agree to these terms, please do not use our service.

section1.title: 1. Acceptance of Terms
section1.content: These Terms of Use ("Terms") govern your access to and use of Bit Scope ("Service", "App", "we", "us", or "our"), a bit-level calculator and number converter application operated by Zuki Apps ("Company"). By downloading, installing, accessing, or using the Service, you agree to be bound by these Terms and our Privacy Policy.
section1.content2: If you are using the Service on behalf of an organization, you represent and warrant that you have the authority to bind that organization to these Terms.

section2.title: 2. Description of Service
section2.content: Bit Scope is a mobile application that provides bit-level number inspection and conversion functionality. The Service includes:
items: Interactive bit editor with visual bit grid
Multiple number format support (8-bit, 16-bit, 32-bit, 64-bit integers and Float16, Float32, Float64)
Number base conversion (Binary, Octal, Decimal, Hexadecimal)
Real-time conversion between formats and bases

section19.title: 19. Contact Information
section19.content: If you have any questions about these Terms, please contact us:

privacy.title: Privacy Policy

section1.title: 1. Information We Collect
section1.content: Bit Scope is designed with privacy in mind. We collect minimal information necessary to provide the service. The app works fully offline and stores all data locally on your device.

section2.title: 2. How We Use Your Information
section2.content: We use the information we collect to provide, maintain, and improve our services. All calculations and conversions are performed locally on your device and are not transmitted to our servers.

section3.title: 3. Information Sharing
section3.content: We do not sell, trade, or otherwise transfer your personal information to third parties. Your calculation data remains on your device and is only accessible to you.

section4.title: 4. Data Security
section4.content: We implement appropriate security measures to protect your information. All data is stored locally on your device in the app's private directory, which is not accessible by other apps.

section5.title: 5. Your Rights
section5.content: You have the right to access, update, or delete your data at any time. You may also uninstall the app at any time, which will remove all locally stored data.

section8.title: 8. Contact Us
section8.content: If you have any questions about this Privacy Policy, please contact us at:

faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.subtitle: Quick answers about features, privacy, and billing.

items.question: What is Bit Scope?
items.answer: Bit Scope is a bit-level calculator and converter for developers. It converts between binary, octal, decimal, and hex; supports IEEE 754 floats; includes bitwise operations; and bundles converters and (with Premium) utilities like CRC and hash.

items.question: Is Bit Scope free?
items.answer: Yes. The calculator, bitwise tools, converters, history, and learning examples are free. Premium is a one-time purchase that removes ads and unlocks the Utilities tab.

items.question: Is Premium a subscription?
items.answer: No. Premium is a one-time in-app purchase. Future Premium-only tools are included without a recurring fee.

items.question: Which platforms are supported?
items.answer: iPhone and iPad (iOS). Android availability follows your Google Play listing. See store links on [zukiapps.com/bit-scope](https://zukiapps.com/bit-scope).

items.question: What languages are supported?
items.answer: 12 base languages with regional variants (e.g. en-US, pt-BR, zh-Hans). Hebrew and Arabic include full RTL layout.

items.question: How do I open the bit canvas?
items.answer: On the Calculator tab, open the bit grid preview or modal (tap the grid / bit visualization control). Tap any bit to toggle it; values update across all representations.

items.question: What is MSB vs LSB bit order?
items.answer: MSB lists bits from most significant to least. LSB reverses the visual order. Choose the view that matches your datasheet or protocol diagram.

items.question: What endianness settings do?
items.answer: Big-endian stores the most significant byte first; little-endian stores the least significant byte first. Bit Scope shows how your integer maps to bytes for each mode.

items.question: Can I use custom bit widths?
items.answer: Yes. Select a preset (8, 16, 32, 64) or enter a custom width (within app limits) for masks and registers that are not power-of-two aligned.

items.question: How does copy work?
items.answer: Tap the copy icon on a result card or tool output. Text is copied without extra spaces (e.g. binary groups may be stripped per field). A checkmark confirms success.

items.question: Which tools are free vs Premium?
items.answer: Free: Base64, color picker, timestamp, value comparator, batch converter—and all calculator features. Premium: CRC, hash, ASCII/Unicode viewer, QR generator, hex dump—and adding utility tools to custom widgets.

items.question: Why does CRC say input is too large?
items.answer: Very large inputs can exceed safe calculation limits on device. Shorten the input or split the data block. Bit Scope shows a clear message instead of failing silently.

items.title: Calculator
items.description: 1. Set width before paste — Pasting `0xFFFF` into 16-bit mode behaves differently than 32-bit. 2. Endian toggle for protocol bugs — Flip BE/LE when a register looks “byte-swapped”. 3. Gray code — Use for encoder / rotary sensor homework or validation. 4. Float switch — Jump INT → F32 to decode IEEE 754 from the same bit pattern. 5. History export — Snapshot a debug session before clearing the chip

items.title: Bit canvas
items.description: 6. Tap don't type — Mask tweaks (set bit 7) are faster on the grid than hex math. 7. Sign bit first — In signed 8-bit, bit 7 is your quick sanity check for negatives.

items.title: Copy workflow
items.description: 8. Checkmark = shipped — Wait for the icon before switching apps. 9. Paste once — Verify hex has no stray spaces before `gcc` or `xxd`.

items.title: Converters
items.description: 10. RAL colors — Firmware UI teams: match enclosure paint chips to RGB for docs. 11. Timestamp now — Tap current-time shortcut (if shown) for log correlation. 12. Comparator — Diff two hex constants side by side before merging branches.

items.title: Utilities (Premium)
items.description: 13. CRC test vectors — Keep `123456789` and `Hello` strings as mental benchmarks. 14. Hex dump import — Paste a snippet from `xxd` to inspect structure. 15. QR for Wi-Fi or URL — Field provisioning without a laptop.

items.title: Widgets & layout
items.description: 16. Three-tool board — Calculator tab + Widgets with Timestamp + Base64 covers 80% of bring-up. 17. iPad rail — Landscape uses side navigation—more vertical space for hex dump.

items.title: Settings
items.description: 18. Dark theme default — Easier on eyes during late firmware sessions; light for outdoor. 19. Language per device — System language vs in-app override for bilingual teams. 20. Learn tab — Send interns to Settings → Learn before asking about two's complement.

items.title: Premium
items.description: 21. One purchase — Restore on new phone before rebuying. 22. Future tools — Premium includes utilities we add later—no upsell tier. --- *Have a tip we should add? zuki.apps.dev@gmail.com*
